key_insight: Cepstral analysis makes Green-Kubo thermal conductivity calculations for MOFs far more stable, faster, and less sensitive to user-chosen parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable, automation-ready prediction of heat transport in porous materials, supporting design of MOFs for thermal management, gas storage, and separation devices.
method: Combine equilibrium Green-Kubo molecular dynamics with cepstral analysis to denoise heat-flux autocorrelation data.
Use machine-learned moment tensor potentials trained on DFT reference data to obtain near ab initio accuracy.
Compare against conventional direct GK analysis across different correlation lengths and sampling times.
evidence_signal: Across three benchmark MOFs (MOF-5, HKUST-1, ZIF-8), the cepstral approach converged robustly within about 1–2 ns and was much less sensitive to ambiguous analysis parameters than direct GK.
watch_item: The workflow still depends on the quality and transferability of the trained machine-learned potential, so poor training data could bias conductivity predictions.
paper_title: Cepstral Analysis to accelerate Green-Kubo thermal conductivity calculations of Metal-Organic Frameworks
